mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising real estate permits signals synchronized expansion in goods and services demand, which compresses supply chain bottlenecks and lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline INDPRO-only model in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in commercial and industrial lending signals credit tightening that restricts corporate capex and consumer borrowing, depressing equity returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 60% of triggered instances despite falling RSAFS, or the predictive relationship reverses direction during periods of quantitative easing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero across all GDP growth deciles.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 95.0, manufacturing capacity utilization signals robust supply-side momentum that reduces input cost inflation and improves profit margins, generating positive equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 95.0, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in unemployment and sustained low federal funds rates reflect accommodative labor and monetary conditions that fuel risk appetite and equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio relative to a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market contractions, a decline in housing starts coupled with flat CPI growth signals weak demand without inflationary pressure, which typically precedes equity drawdowns as earnings expectations are revised downward over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal's predictive accuracy deteriorates significantly when the Federal Reserve implements forward guidance interventions.
